QF.id: Retirement Planning Products: National Pension System (NPS)_21

question.text: How many times per year can an NPS subscriber change their investment option (Active/Auto choice)?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Subscribers can change their investment option (between Active and Auto, or within Auto lifecycle funds) up to 4 times per year. **The Trap:** Changing Pension Fund Manager is allowed only once per year, different from investment option changes (4 times). These are separate limits. **Cross-Exam Context:** Investment option change: 4 times/year. PFM change: once/year. Under Corporate Model, up to 3 PFMs can be chosen for Active Choice. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Advise clients to use investment option changes strategically, typically at the start of each quarter based on market conditions.
